input_artifacts: Approved Enhanced SRS JSON if available

introduction.purpose: Describe the software design for the Login feature based on the approved SRS.
scope: Allow users to log in using email and password.
Validate user credentials against stored records.
Generate and return a JWT authentication token upon successful login.
Provide a mechanism/link for users to initiate the 'Forgot Password' flow.
out_of_scope: Password reset via email verification (Only the initiation flow is in scope).
Profile management or account settings updates.

definitions.definition: Software Design Specification generated using an IEEE 1016-style design description structure.

design_context.business_goal: Allow registered users to securely access their account.
design_context.feature_boundary: This SDS covers only the Login feature and excludes unrelated features.
assumptions: The user account already exists in the database before login is attempted.
A secure mechanism for hashing passwords (e.g., bcrypt) is available and implemented.
The backend service responsible for user data is available and functional.
Revision requested by human_user: Add forgot username requirement and related acceptance criteria.
Fallback revision was used because LLM revision failed: Expecting ',' delimiter: line 167 column 5 (char 5436)

constraints: Must use MERN stack (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js).
Authentication must utilize JWT for token generation.
The application must adhere to the MVC architectural pattern.

non_functional_requirements.description: The login API response time must be fast, ideally under 500ms, even under peak load.
The user interface must be fully responsive and functional across major desktop and mobile screen sizes.
Error messages must be clear, non-technical, and guide the user toward resolution (e.g., 'Invalid email or password').

risks.risk: Credential stuffing or brute force attacks.
risks.mitigation: Implement rate limiting and account lockout mechanisms.

risks.description: This fallback revision should be reviewed carefully before approval.

design_tradeoffs: Design is derived directly from the approved SRS to preserve traceability and feature scope.
Design is kept feature-scoped to preserve feature-by-feature SDLC development.
Internal generation or repair details are kept in backend logs and are not exposed in the approval-ready SDS.

architecture_overview.architecture_rationale: Use mvc to keep the Login feature separated into presentation, business logic, and data responsibilities where applicable.
architecture_overview.frontend_overview: Frontend design should provide the user-facing entry points, inputs, states, and feedback required by the approved SRS.
architecture_overview.backend_overview: Backend design should process feature requests, enforce business rules, validate inputs, and return structured responses.
architecture_overview.data_overview: Data design should support the data requirements defined in the approved SRS.
architecture_overview.integration_overview: Integration design should follow the API expectations and dependencies listed in the approved SRS.

main_interactions: Customer interacts with the Login feature.
Admin interacts with the Login feature.
The system must allow registered users to authenticate using a valid email address and password.
The system must validate the provided credentials against the user database.
Upon successful login, the system must generate and return a secure JSON Web Token (JWT) to the client.
The system must provide a link or mechanism to initiate the 'Forgot Password' process.

frontend_modules.name: LoginFrontendModule
frontend_modules.responsibility: Handle frontend responsibilities for the Login feature.
derived_from: Must include fields for Email and Password, and a Submit button.
Must be visible near the login form.

backend_modules.name: LoginBackendModule
backend_modules.responsibility: Handle backend responsibilities for the Login feature.
derived_from: The system must allow registered users to authenticate using a valid email address and password.
The system must validate the provided credentials against the user database.
Upon successful login, the system must generate and return a secure JSON Web Token (JWT) to the client.
The system must provide a link or mechanism to initiate the 'Forgot Password' process.
Email must conform to standard email regex pattern.
Password must be at least 8 characters long.

domain_services.name: LoginService
domain_services.responsibility: Apply business rules for the Login feature.

data_modules.name: LoginDataModule
data_modules.responsibility: Manage data access required by the feature.
derived_from: Requires secure storage and retrieval of hashed passwords and unique email addresses.

fields.name: Authentication Token
fields.description: A secure token required for subsequent API calls.

fields.name: Error Message
fields.description: Displayed to the user upon failed login attempts.

data_entities.name: User Credentials
data_entities.purpose: Requires secure storage and retrieval of hashed passwords and unique email addresses.
indexes_or_constraints: Protected value storage required where applicable.

storage_rules: Requires secure storage and retrieval of hashed passwords and unique email addresses.

data_validation_rules.rule: Email must conform to standard email regex pattern.
Password must be at least 8 characters long.

main_success_flow.description: System validates the request according to approved SRS rules.
System returns the expected result or a clear error response.

alternative_flows.description: Given valid credentials (email and password), the user must be redirected to the main dashboard and receive a valid JWT.
When the user clicks the 'Forgot Password' link, they must be directed to the password recovery initiation page.

exception_flows.description: Given invalid credentials, the system must display a generic, clear error message and prevent access.

state_changes.description: Upon successful login, the system must generate and return a secure JSON Web Token (JWT) to the client.
Given valid credentials (email and password), the user must be redirected to the main dashboard and receive a valid JWT.

validation_errors.condition: Email must conform to standard email regex pattern.
validation_errors.handling: Return a clear validation message and prevent invalid processing.

validation_errors.condition: Password must be at least 8 characters long.
validation_errors.handling: Return a clear validation message and prevent invalid processing.

business_errors.condition: Given invalid credentials, the system must display a generic, clear error message and prevent access.
business_errors.handling: Return a clear, user-friendly error and prevent the invalid action.

authorization_errors.condition: Given invalid credentials, the system must display a generic, clear error message and prevent access.
authorization_errors.handling: Prevent unauthorized access and return an authorization-safe response.

system_errors: Unexpected system errors should return safe generic messages without exposing internal details.
user_message_rules: Error messages must be clear, non-technical, and guide the user toward resolution (e.g., 'Invalid email or password').

authentication_design: Apply authentication behaviour required by the SRS and selected architecture.
sensitive_data_rules: Sensitive values must not be exposed in responses, logs, or generated artifacts.

risk_mitigations.risk: Credential stuffing or brute force attacks.
risk_mitigations.mitigation: Implement rate limiting and account lockout mechanisms.

risk_mitigations.risk: {'description': 'This fallback revision should be reviewed carefully before approval.'}
risk_mitigations.mitigation: Apply suitable mitigation based on project security policy.

usability.description: The user interface must be fully responsive and functional across major desktop and mobile screen sizes.
usability.design_decision: Design frontend/user-facing behaviour to satisfy the stated usability expectation.

usability.description: Error messages must be clear, non-technical, and guide the user toward resolution (e.g., 'Invalid email or password').
usability.design_decision: Design frontend/user-facing behaviour to satisfy the stated usability expectation.
